items.body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## đźŽŻ Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue documents a documentation gap in the `Azure.ResourceManager.Advisor` package after the beta.6 release added new resource types from the `2025-05-01-preview` API version. It requests README updates, inline code snippet wiring, and alignment with the canonical template.

<details>
<summary>đź“‹ Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Advisor` (1.0.0-beta.6)
**Affected API:** `AdvisorAssessmentResource`, `AdvisorResiliencyReviewResource`, `AdvisorTriageResource`, `AdvisorTriageRecommendationResource`
**Scenarios:**
- New resource types added in beta.6 are not mentioned in the README
- No inline `C# Snippet:` examples exist; `tests/Samples/` files lack `#region Snippet:` markers
- The `Key concepts` section only links to general docs rather than describing individual resource types
- A non-standard `## Documentation` section exists that deviates from the canonical README template

**Root ask:** Update README with resource descriptions and inline snippet placeholders, add `#region Snippet:` markers to sample files, remove the non-standard section, and run `Update-Snippets.ps1` to wire everything together.
</details>

<details>
<summary>đź”Ž Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue; no runtime bug is described. The gap was automatically detected by a workflow that compared the updated generated code (PR #58281 by `@ArthurMa1978`) against the existing README.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/advisor/Azure.ResourceManager.Advisor/README.md` and compare against `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` (canonical template reference)
2. Inspect `sdk/advisor/Azure.ResourceManager.Advisor/tests/Samples/` for existing sample files and add `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` markers
3. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 advisor` after adding markers to verify snippet injection into README
4. Run `dotnet build sdk/advisor/Azure.ResourceManager.Advisor/` and `dotnet test sdk/advisor/Azure.ResourceManager.Advisor/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` to confirm no compilation errors
</details>

<details>
<summary>đźŹ·ď¸Ź Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` â€” The affected package is `Azure.ResourceManager.Advisor`, which is clearly a management-plane SDK (`Azure.ResourceManager.*` prefix)
- **Service:** `Advisor` â€” Explicitly named in the issue title, body, package name, and service directory path (`sdk/advisor/`)
- **Confidence:** High â€” Package name and service directory are explicitly stated; no ambiguity between categories or services
</details>

<details>
<summary>đź‘Ą Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Mgmt` (line 903) â€” Matched the Mgmt catch-all because no `%Advisor %Mgmt` compound entry exists; all more-specific `%<Something> %Mgmt` entries further down the file did not include "Advisor"
- **AzureSdkOwners:** `@ArthurMa1978`
- **ServiceOwners:** none listed in the `%Mgmt` catch-all entry; `%Advisor` (line 533) has ServiceOwners `@mojayara `@Prasanna-Padmanabhan`` but that entry was not reached because `%Mgmt` caught it first during bottom-to-top scan
- **Routing action:** Assigned `@ArthurMa1978` (single AzureSdkOwner from `%Mgmt` catch-all); no "needs-team-attention" added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported)
- **Scan notes:** All `%<Service> %Mgmt` compound entries (lines 954â€“1221) were scanned first and skipped because none included "Advisor"; `%ARM %Mgmt` (line 915) skipped because issue lacks the "ARM" label; `%Mgmt` (line 903) was the first full match
</details>
